title: barleria parviflora
intro: Barleria parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the "Small-flowered Firecracker" due to its vibrant red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Barleria parviflora include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting its essential oils to use in aromatherapy.
